folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 20 - Wright Case
gm_summary: Abigail bought a copy of The King in Yellow in February 1995, shared it with the other tenants, discovered the Night Floors in early May, and used them to build her shrine. In the truth of Part One, she is lost to the Night World and has moved toward Carcosa. She cannot be recovered during this operation.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 20 - Wright Case
gm_summary: Thomas’s recollections anchor several important facts: Abigail found the Red Book while shopping with him, her mother Ellen died warning him that “He’s coming for my baby,” and Thomas himself found the shrine on 4 JUN 1995. He is emotionally credible, but the truth he carries is larger than he can understand.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 10 - Delta Green & Law
gm_summary: Bloom is part of the newer Delta Green culture of the 1990s. He is not the occult revelation of this chapter; he is the operational spine that gets the Agents into the building and gives them a way to interpret early danger.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 10 - Delta Green & Law
gm_summary: Giuradanda is one of the safest and strongest allies in Part One. He is grounded, competent, religious, and potentially replacement-Agent material if the table needs him.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 20 - Wright Case
gm_summary: Lechance has no knowledge of the Night Floors or the building’s deeper reality. Her role is to confirm the day-world situation: the tenants stopped paying, no one will speak to her, and ARTLIFE is quietly eager to clear the building for renovation.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 30 - Macallistar Tenants
gm_summary: Manuel has been drawn deeply into the Night Floors. He recorded Wright’s apartment, hid clues in his plant base, worked in the basement studio, and describes Wright as having “moved on” or “figured it out.” At night he may be found making tapes of Wright’s apartment, working on paintings alone while audibly conversing with someone unseen, or playing ball with the Dog.

canonical_name: OP1 - NPC - Arthur and Elaine Manuel [GM]
folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 40 - Families & Contacts
gm_summary: Their key value is as external witnesses. On 31 MAR 1995, after dark, they were turned away from the Macallistar by a “man with a suitcase and a large grey dog” who said Thomas no longer lived there.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 30 - Macallistar Tenants
gm_summary: Carun is one of the strongest clue carriers in the building. His last outside call went to Carmen Wagner. At night he casually accepts the Night Floors as if they are normal, insists Wright still lives upstairs on Six, and guards his apartment with hairs over openings to detect intruders.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 40 - Families & Contacts
gm_summary: Wagner holds the 19 APR answering-machine message and has also been getting strange recurring night calls from an unknown man who says, “Start with Peter Devoras,” before listing names.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 30 - Macallistar Tenants
gm_summary: Abigail’s copy of The King in Yellow ended up on Vanfitz’s shelves after Wright shared it. Vanfitz is one of the clearest routes from the daytime investigation into the Night Floors, because her apartment becomes an access point by night.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 30 - Macallistar Tenants
gm_summary: Post is less a clue fountain than a pressure point. He is deeply unstable and increasingly dangerous, especially by night.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 50 - Night Floors Residents
gm_summary: Langford crossed into the Night Floors on 20 MAR 1995 while working the cable box at the top-floor stair landing just as day turned to night. He has survived by hiding, scavenging old food and drink, and avoiding the other residents.

canonical_name: OP1 - NPC - Henri de Calvados Castaigne [GM]
folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 50 - Night Floors Residents
gm_summary: Castaigne claims Carcosa as his place of origin and dreams of kingship. He is one of the better social bridges deeper into Night World logic, but he is also vain, dangerous, and consumed by bottle- and throne-oriented obsessions.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 30 - People / Groups / 50 - Night Floors Residents
gm_summary: He is not a stable witness so much as a recurring encounter-form. Agents below higher Corruption mostly catch him only in peripheral glimpses; those who can perceive him more clearly still cannot catch him.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 10 - Core Building
gm_summary: By day it looks merely strange; by night it opens into the Night Floors and the tenants live double lives.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 40 - External Investigation Sites
gm_summary: Gives the clean day-world truth: tenancy, late rent, and ARTLIFE’s quiet desire to empty the building for renovation.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 10 - Core Building
gm_summary: Central clue location; prolonged exposure affects Agents; the apartment is the clearest physical sign that the building is already infected.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
gm_summary: Contains clear signs of surveillance, fixation, and night-side leakage even when Manuel denies anything is wrong.

canonical_name: OP1 - Location - Thomas Manuel’s Basement Studio [GM]
folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 30 - Building Subareas
gm_summary: Holds “My Great Work,” linseed oil for “Sami,” and paintings that later echo real-world horrors.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
gm_summary: Carun is altering or channeling material tied to the play; his apartment supports the last-call clue path and paranoia signs.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
gm_summary: By day it contains Wright’s battered Red Book; by night an archway opens to endless book-rooms of the Night Floors.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 20 - Tenant Apartments
gm_summary: By night he becomes far more dangerous; the apartment is more a pressure-cooker than a clue cache.

canonical_name: OP1 - Location - Top-Floor Stair Landing / Roof Door [GM]
folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 10 - Core Building
gm_summary: Langford vanished here while working the cable box at the day/night threshold; this is also tied to Wright’s 1994 mugging near the roof door.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 50 - Night Floors
gm_summary: The lost and the mad live here as dark caricatures; Wright cannot be recovered here in Part One, only understood.

folder_path: OP1 - The Night Floors / 20 - Locations / 50 - Night Floors
gm_summary: Manuel places Wright’s going-away party here; it is a stable enough named anchor to warrant its own location entry.
